title: Alaska Native Village Municipal Lands Restoration Act of 2025
short_title: Alaska Native Village Municipal Lands Restoration Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law helps Alaska Native village corporations get back land they had to give away to the state. Under an old law, these corporations had to give certain lands to the state to hold for possible future city governments, even if those cities were never created. Now these corporations can get their land back if both the corporation and the village residents agree to it. The law also says these corporations do not have to give away any more land to the state for this reason in the future.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Alaska Native Village Municipal Lands Restoration Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Reversion of Certain Land Conveyed in Trust to the State of Alaska
sections.text: This section changes the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act. The changes affect how Native village corporations handle land that was given to Alaska in trust to possibly create city governments. The main changes are that Native village corporations will no longer be required to give any more land to the state for creating future city governments. If a village corporation already gave land to the state for this purpose but no city government was actually created, the village corporation and village residents can ask for that land to come back to them. When land comes back to the village corporation, it must still respect any existing rights that other people have to use the land, like leases or roads. The village corporation must also take over any responsibilities that came with the trust agreement when they get the land back.
